column17: If your "must-have" list starts and ends with great tacos, you’ve found your perfect home match. Around here, we believe a "prime location" is best measured by how quickly you can get to your favorite taco stand! But while the best local spots are just up the road, I think we can all agree nothing beats homegrown flavor. With a yard ready for a salsa garden and a kitchen built for blending ingredients with love, here you get the best of both worlds. Inside, this 3-bedroom, 2-bathroom house feels surprisingly spacious. The bedrooms offer plenty of room to unwind, while the covered front porch is the ultimate spot to sit back and enjoy the neighborhood breeze. Updated, clean, and low-maintenance, this home is designed so you can spend less time on chores and more time soaking up the sunshine and fresh air. Between the modern updates and the welcoming feel, this home is a fresh start that’s ready for you to move in and garnish with your own personal style. When it’s time to leave the house, you’ll find the convenience is hard to beat. You have a straight shot to I-35 and easy access to Temple College, the mall, and local hospitals. Even with all that convenience, you're even closer to the sprawling green spaces at James Wilson Park and several others. It is the perfect setup for staying tucked away while remaining totally connected. Living here might mean you’re the gatekeeper to the best flavors in town, but most importantly, you’re the owner of a refreshed home that fits your lifestyle as perfectly as your favorite taco order.

column17: This rare find in Southwest Temple has so much to offer and covers three lots.  A lovely, classic home with two adjacent lots for a total of .693 acres nestled on a creek, in town, and conveniently located to shopping and/or I-35.  This stately home has a foyer as you enter, with a woodburning fireplace as the focal point of the room.  A wall of built-in bookcases will accommodate your TV in this room.  The dining room has crown-molding and chair railing around the room. The kitchen is open to the living room and has granite countertops, a pantry, with black and stainless-steel appliances.  The large eat-in kitchen area overlooks the backyard.  The primary bedroom is on one side of the house with a door opening to the patio, and the primary bathroom has double sinks, a garden tub, and separate shower.  The other two downstairs bedrooms are on the other side of the house with a full guest bathroom.  Upstairs there is another bedroom with its own half bath, plus a game room that is plumbed with a sink and refrigerator.  Two doors open to attic areas with a lot of floored storage space.  This home has a central vacuum system and could accommodate a multi-generational family situation.  One real bonus to this property is the additional two-car garage and/or workshop, approximately 24' x 24', tastefully coordinated and matching the home.  The house already has an oversized side-entry garage approximately 23' x 24'.  There is a lot of parking space on this property for parking numerous vehicles.  Finally, the last lot has a huge live oak that must be at least 100 years old, and there is a park-like feel to this added green space.  A wonderful home, an extra two-car garage, and a park of your own describes this lovely property on three lots.  A rare find.

column17: Have you ever wanted to own a piece of history? This lovely 1907 Queen Anne Victorian farm house is your rare opportunity. Registered with both the federal and state historical associations, this four bedroom, 2 1/2 bath home offers two living areas, a huge kitchen with breakfast area, a spacious dining room, a butler’s pantry and two floors of wraparound porches. A detached three car garage offers room for a workshop or a hobbyist to enjoy being creative. The home is set on .38 acres on a lot with mature pecan and oak trees.  The house is known as the Ferguson Home, James was governor before his wife, Miriam. He built this house for her when they were living in Belton. Because she was a gardener, he was able to lure her to Temple with a mason jar of soil from the land. He founded the Ferguson bank and ran for office. She followed him as the first woman elected to the office of governor in this country. Two historical pictures come with the house, you will be able to see them on the walls. There is also other photos taken over the years and memorabilia that the new resident will have upon move in. The blue paint on the porch ceilings have proven to keep wasps away, as the house has been well maintained top to bottom. Professional photos to come soon as well as yard sign. Now priced to sell as Seller is downsizing and searching for a new residence. My last reduction was a typo. The house is in excellent shape and has been maintained very well.

column17: Check out this stunner! This home has fresh interior paint . Discover a bright and open interior with plenty of natural light and a neutral color palette, complimented by a fireplace. Step into the kitchen, complete with an eye catching stylish backsplash. You won’t want to leave the serene primary suite, the perfect space to relax. Additional bedrooms provide nice living or office space. Take advantage of the extended counter space in the primary bathroom complete with double sinks and under sink storage. Step outside to the pristinely maintained fenced in backyard with pool, great for entertaining. If the shade is more your style, hang out under the covered sitting area. Don't miss this incredible opportunity.
